blocks.content: The thing about a promise to Daisy May Jones was that she never had to remind you of it. It just sat there in your chest all night.
Kit was up before his alarm. This was not a thing that happened.

He lay there with the ceiling fan going overhead and the summer heat already pressing through the window screen, and he thought about the Henderson property, and the creaky floors, and the saddle Eli kept insisting was in the barn.
He got up. Got dressed. Grabbed his bike from the porch and rode two blocks north to Magnolia Street.
Daisy was already on the front steps. She had a granola bar in one hand and her hair was still loose from sleep, and she looked up when she heard the bike with no surprise at all.
She stood and finished the granola bar and folded the wrapper small and tucked it in her pocket, and came down the steps and picked up her bike from the side of the porch.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We look before we touch anything. We stay where we can see each other. If we hear anything that isn't us, we go.
He had forgotten there was an and.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We don't split up. I know.
They rode to the corner. Eli was already there, practically vibrating, and Savannah was beside him with her arms crossed.
